description_long: This object is a large, irregularly shaped cluster of gray rocks, with several rounded boulders fused together to form a single mass. Embedded within the main body are a few angular, dark bluish-gray stone fragments that contrast with the lighter, weathered surface of the surrounding rock. The surface texture is rough and uneven, with visible crevices, bulges, and natural indentations typical of weathered stone. The coloration ranges from light to medium gray, with some areas appearing slightly mottled or marbled, and the darker inclusions have a subtle sheen. The overall form is bulky and squat, with no sharp edges, making it appear stable and heavy. This type of rock formation could serve as a natural barrier or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A bulky, irregular cluster of light gray rocks with embedded dark bluish-gray stone fragments and a rough, weathered surface. The mass is squat and heavy, with rounded forms and natural crevices.
description_view_0: The front view shows the largest rounded boulder with a prominent dark stone fragment partially embedded at the top right.
description_view_1: This side reveals more of the angular dark stone, which juts out from the lighter rock mass and highlights the layered structure.
description_view_2: From this angle, the dark stone fragments are more pronounced, wrapping around the central boulders and creating a banded appearance.
description_view_3: This view emphasizes the smoother, lighter gray surface with only small portions of the dark stone visible, making the mass appear more monolithic.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped public space
